title: polygala coriacea
intro: Polygala coriacea, also known as False Oregon Grape or Five-leaved Milkwort, is a perennial herbaceous plant native to the southeastern United States. The plant is rich in medicinal properties, particularly its roots, which contain alkaloids and other compounds that have been traditionally used to treat various health conditions. The health benefits of Polygala coriacea include its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which can help to soothe digestive issues and reduce fever. Herbal preparations of the plant include teas, tinctures, and infusions, which can be used to treat a range of health issues, including digestive problems and skin conditions.
